narrative_blocks.header: Co-compiler of the Whittlesey Memorials family history
narrative_blocks.body: Charles served as Assistant Corresponding Secretary of the Whittlesey Association and was one of the compilers of the 'Whittlesey Memorials' — the family-history compendium that became foundational source material for the 1898 expanded Whittlesey Genealogy. His co-laborer Henry Martyn Whittlesey had earlier published the 1855 first edition.

narrative_blocks.header: Wholesale and retail druggist of New Haven
narrative_blocks.body: Charles graduated Yale Medical College in 1843 and built a wholesale and retail drug practice at New Haven Connecticut. He had removed to New Haven in 1839 and remained there until his death in 1878.

narrative_blocks.header: Deacon and Treasurer at New Haven Center Church
narrative_blocks.body: Charles served the Center Church of New Haven as Deacon, Treasurer, and Sunday-school Superintendent. The Center Church was the same First Church of Christ congregation Rev. Chauncey Whittlesey had pastored from 1758 to 1787 — a kinship of place across the generations of Whittleseys.

INST122.name: Yale School of Medicine (Medical Institution of Yale College)
INST122.notes:
Founded 1810 as the Medical Institution of Yale College, the fourth oldest medical school in the United States.
